author: śrī⋅caṇḍīdāsa
verses:
sukhera lāgiyā, e ghara bā̐dhinu,
āgune puḍiyā gela
amiyā⋅sāgare, sināna karite,
sakali garala bhela

sakhi, ki mora kapāle lekhi
śītala baliyā, cā̐da sevinu,
bhānura kiraṇa dekhi

ucala baliyā, acale caḍinu,
paḍinu agādha jale
lachamī cāhite, dāridrya beḍhala,
māṇika hārānu hele

nagara basālāma, sāgara bā̐dhilāma,
māṇika pābāra āśe
sāgara śukāla, māṇika lukāla,
abhāgī⋅karama⋅doṣe

piyāsa lāgiyā, jalada sevinu,
vajara paḍiyā gela
kahe caṇḍīdāsa, śyāmera pirīti,
marame rahala śela
en_translation:
For the sake of happiness, I built this house, but it burned down in a fire. I went to bathe in the ocean of nectar, but everything turned to poison.

O friend! What is written in my fate? Thinking it would be cool, I worshipped the moon, but instead, I saw the scorching rays of the sun.

Thinking it was high ground, I climbed the mountain, only to fall into deep water. Seeking wealth, my poverty instead increased, and I carelessly lost the jewel I had.

I established a city and bound the ocean, hoping to find a precious gem. But the ocean dried up and the jewel remained hidden, all due to the fault of my unfortunate past actions.

Feeling thirsty, I worshipped the cloud, but instead, a thunderbolt struck. Chaṇḍīdāsa says: "My love for Lord Śyāma has remained like a spear in my heart."
